problems.title: Can the conjecture supply a non-circular, precise criterion for when an internal
problems.summary: Can the conjecture supply a non-circular, precise criterion for when an internal state genuinely functions as a model with correctness conditions, rather than merely participating in a causal control
problems.full_text: Can the conjecture supply a non-circular, precise criterion for when an internal state genuinely functions as a model with correctness conditions, rather than merely participating in a causal control loop? The central tension is whether 'model-bearing', 'wrong about the world', and 'used as informative' can be defined without already presupposing intentional notions.

problems.title: How can 'irreducibly perspectival' or 'loss-sensitive to decomposition' be made
problems.summary: How can 'irreducibly perspectival' or 'loss-sensitive to decomposition' be made precise enough to distinguish conscious from nonconscious systems without collapsing into vagueness or circularity? The
problems.full_text: How can 'irreducibly perspectival' or 'loss-sensitive to decomposition' be made precise enough to distinguish conscious from nonconscious systems without collapsing into vagueness or circularity? The conjecture hinges on a boundary property, but it is unclear what formal criterion would measure unified self-world modeling, privileged point of view, and decomposition failure across brains, AI systems, split-brain cases, and distributed collectives.
